response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses triggered by proteins found in peanuts  
These reactions can range from mild symptoms such as hives to severe reactions such as anaphylaxis  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins  
Peanut proteins can be present in food or environmental sources even in underwater environments  
The body's immune system does not differentiate between environments where the allergen is present  

Sec. 2  
Allergic reactions occur when the immune system mistakenly identifies a harmless substance as harmful  
This process is not affected by the physical location of the individual  
Underwater living does not prevent the ingestion or inhalation of peanut allergens  
Medical treatment for peanut allergies involves avoiding the allergen and using epinephrine in case of severe reactions  
Therefore people with peanut allergies cannot live underwater because the allergen can still be present and trigger a reaction
